payload.answer: recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman because the system determined mem_new represents the superseding (more current/active) information for that entity-context pair, and therefore filtered out mem_old as superseded.
payload.conflict_classification: supersession_conflict (newer/active memory overrides older memory for the same entity+context)
payload.decision: recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman and filtered mem_old because mem_new supersedes mem_old according to the system’s freshness/supersession rule.

entity_resolution.action: resolved as the same entity (Sam) with the same key context (Goldman) across mem_new and mem_old
entity_resolution.reason: Both candidate memories refer to Sam in the Goldman context, so the conflict resolver treated them as overlapping versions of the same underlying memory.

payload.intent: Explain the recall conflict behavior for Sam from Goldman, distinguishing why one memory was selected as current and another was filtered as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new for Sam from Goldman was returned as the selected/current memory.
mem_old for Sam from Goldman was filtered out because it is superseded by mem_new (i.e., the system judged mem_old as not the latest/active version).

details: Selected path: mem_new returned as current
Filtered path: mem_old excluded due to supersession determination

repair_options: Verify that mem_new and mem_old are keyed consistently to the same entity+context (Sam + Goldman) so the supersession logic can apply correctly.
If mem_old should remain relevant, adjust tagging/metadata (e.g., context keys, timestamps, or supersession markers) so it is not treated as overridden.
If mem_new is incorrect, update or retract it and ensure the system can correctly promote mem_old (or another alternative) as the current memory.
